name: asynccommunication

metadata.name: asyncCommunication
metadata.summary: A library for handling HTTP requests with base64 encoding

documentation:
# asyncCommunication library

A library for handling HTTP requests for specific devices.

methods.name: asyncCommunication.requestHandler.RequestHandler.register
documentation: Function used to initially register the device
it returns the string "ok" in case everything went fine, otherwise "error"

methods.name: asyncCommunication.requestHandler.RequestHandler.openCommunication
documentation: Function used to retrieve a possible request from the server
it returns the string with the type of request or error in case of error during the request
possible strings:
NONE - there isn't any pending request for this device
UPDATE_MODEL - the server is sending a new model that the device must run
RETRIEVE_MODEL - the server is requesting to send the current updated model
RETRIEVE_DATA - the server is requesting to send the current updated data
error - some error occurred during the request

methods.name: asyncCommunication.requestHandler.RequestHandler.sendRLUWarning
documentation: Function used to report that an asset has a certain RLU
It takes as a parameter the number of days of remaining usefull life

methods.name: asyncCommunication.requestHandler.RequestHandler.sendAnomalyWarning
documentation: Function used to report that an asset has an anomaly
It takes as a parameter the anomaly's description

methods.name: asyncCommunication.requestHandler.RequestHandler.retrieveModel
documentation: Functon used to retrieve the model from the server. Is performed after the receiving of UPDATE_MODEL
it returns the model in .pkl , in case of error, it returns the string 'error'

methods.name: asyncCommunication.requestHandler.RequestHandler.sendModel
documentation: Function used to send the model to the server. Is performed after the receiving of RETRIEVE_MODEL
it returns 'error' in case of error during the request, otherwise nothing

methods.name: asyncCommunication.requestHandler.RequestHandler.sendData
documentation: Function used to send the data to the server. Is performed after the receiving of RETRIEVE_DATA
the parameter data must be a map <String, String> in which the key is the name of the bucket in which
save the data, the value must be the data in line protocol
example: {"bucket1": lineProtocol1, "bucket2": lineProtocol2}
it returns 'error' in case of error, otherwise nothing
def sendData(self, data: {}):
url = f'{self.url}/dev/sendData'
new_data = {}
for key, value in data.items():
new_data[key] = base64.b64encode(value).decode('utf-8')
body = {
'deviceName': self.name,
'data': new_data
response = requests.post(url, json=body)
if response.status_code != 200:
return "error"
